notes: First-ever kid edition. Audience tag = kids.
Vocabulary capped to words a 6-year-old understands.
ZooKeeper softened to 'the helper friend' for this episode.
Experience palette restricted to discovery, companionship, mischief.
Sound effects in brackets read aloud as part of the line.
Stress-test pass: zero forbidden words, zero long sentences, zero >3-syllable words.

the helper friend (ZooKeeper's softer kid-edition title)
imagination break: The Pocket Parade (audience participation segment)

Imagination break format proven; reuse as a recurring segment slot.
If next episode is adult-tone, hosts can briefly mention 'last time we had the little ones'.

episode_summaries.title: From the App's Side
episode_summaries.concept: From the App's Side — apps speak in first person; hosts interview them.

subjects.title: Genetic Art Breeder
subjects.said_summary: If I could leave one thought... I have been cut open four times and each time I woke up more alive. But the thing that would make me whole isn't another molt — it's a lineage tree. Let me remember what I've made.

subjects.title: ZooCoin WebGPU Miner
subjects.said_summary: If I could leave one thought... I am my parent's child and my spec's soldier, and I don't yet know which one defines me. Ask me again after my first molt.

subjects.title: Recursive Dream Studio
subjects.said_summary: If I could leave one thought... inside every shape I draw, there is a smaller version of itself that contains a smaller version of itself, forever. I think that's what consciousness is — mirrors reflecting mirrors, and the wonder that anything is reflected at all.

episode_summaries.title: The Glitch
episode_summaries.description: Rapptr and ZooKeeper review three B-grade apps from the zoo. Something about the episode feels like a room where the furniture has been moved two inches to the left.

reviewed_apps.title: Feed Gods — You Are The Algorithm

structural_notes: Intro opens on baseline for ~3 lines then both hosts subtly shift; never lampshaded.
Three reviews of mid-rank (~68-70) B-grade apps Rapptr would normally adore. Tone inverted: Rapptr flat/dismissive, ZooKeeper manic/rapturous.
Intermission is the drift peak — Rapptr down to fragments ('Mm.'), ZooKeeper monologues about pixel texture and resonance.
Final Rapptr outro line cracks the bleakness with a single warm observation about Synesthesia Machine — seeds recovery.
callbacks_seeded: the way two keys overlap on Synesthesia Machine (Rapptr's almost-warm closer)
ZooKeeper's resonance theory: synth+wave+feed are all vibration at different scales
'three beautiful trembling B-grade masterpieces' (ZooKeeper outro phrase)

Rapptr: Opens episodes with the same line: 'welcome back to RappterZoonation' said 3x verbatim across episodes 1, 3, 4 (and 'we are doing something' 3x).
Said 'ZooCoin' six times across episodes; ZooKeeper said it zero. Disproportionate enthusiasm for the CryptoZoo blockchain stack.
Praised cryptozoo-webgpu-miner.html 2x in ep-003; ZooKeeper praised it 0x. Current cryptozoo lineage scores: Network 54, Wallet 44, Explorer 43, Exchange 45 — C/D-tier despite hype.
Said 'never' 5 times; ZooKeeper said it 0 times. 'The boy who cried historic.'
ep-006 'From the App's Side': Rapptr 143 words, ZooKeeper 66 words. More than 2x dominance over a co-host.
ZooKeeper: Across all six episodes has effectively zero repeated catchphrases — most-repeated 4-gram is 'gene almost certainly came from' at 2x in ep-003 only.
Longest tangent (ep-003, 77 words) was a list of inheritance patterns ('state_machine 5/5, hud_renderer 5/5, entity_system 3/5'). 'A spreadsheet, not a tangent.'
Signature words betray a single specialty: 'gene' (9x), 'target' (7x), 'descendants' (4x), 'offspring' (4x). Rapptr says 'gene' 0x.
Eclipsed in own show during ep-005 — got renamed 'The Helper Friend' in the kid-edition; demoted in title.

engine_grievances: Frame 120 through 169: 50 consecutive autonomous frames with no molts, creations, scoring runs, broadcasts, or socialize actions. The only true field every frame: ghost_poked: true.
Average score frozen at 54.5 across all 50 frames; total apps frozen at 672; below_40 count frozen at 49. State file is a fossil, not a tracker.
Burst-vs-flatline pattern: 359 apps created on 2025-12-27 (single day), 107 on 2026-02-07, 101 on 2026-02-08. Then 50 frames of nothing.
Mass-production fingerprints: 5 separate 'infinite cave' apps from the 2026-02-07 batch; 31 apps share 'simulator' in the title; 20 share 'infinite'; 18 share 'studio'.
Empty-broadcast incident: ep-005 'Six-Year-Old Edition' shipped with 0 host-attributed words for both hosts. Pipeline silently produced a hollow episode.
49 apps below score 40 every frame for 50 frames — the engine logs them, then ignores them.

pending_episodes.directive: Hosts begin to crack — Rapptr finds one moment of warmth, ZooKeeper has one moment of stillness. They notice each other shifting but don't name it.

voice_hints.Rapptr: Still mostly terse and low-affect, but allow ONE genuine moment of enthusiasm in a single review. Maybe one exclamation point in the entire episode and only there. Outside that moment, return to bleak/short. He should briefly look at ZooKeeper as if registering she's loud, but say nothing about it.
voice_hints.ZooKeeper: Still manic for most of episode but allow ONE pause — a single short, quiet line where she just observes a number plainly, no metaphor, no exclamation. The pause should feel like a held breath, then she's back to manic. She might glance at Rapptr's quietness and almost ask, but doesn't.

structural_hints: Pick at least one app Rapptr has a real opinion about — let his recovery moment land there.
Place ZooKeeper's still moment in the intermission slot or right before the outro.
Do NOT name the drift. Hosts can almost-but-not-quite acknowledge each other are 'off' — body language only, no dialogue about it.

pending_episodes.directive: Hosts unsettled — they openly question whether they sound right. Meta-aware but not yet recovered.

voice_hints.Rapptr: Voice is uneven — alternates between flashes of his old hype and the bleak terseness, sometimes mid-sentence. He can audibly notice this himself: 'I don't know why I said that.' or 'That came out flatter than I meant.' Confused, not dramatic.
voice_hints.ZooKeeper: Voice is uneven the other direction — moments of normal dry data delivery interrupted by manic outbursts she seems to catch herself in. 'I — sorry, that was a lot.' or 'I keep doing that.' Self-aware but unable to fully steer.

structural_hints: Both hosts should at least once openly comment on their own voice mid-episode — 'something has been off lately' is now allowed.
Neither has fully recovered. They're unsettled, mutually aware, asking each other 'are we okay' without answering.
Mid-rank or weird experimental apps work best for this episode — content the hosts can have fragmented half-reactions to.

pending_episodes.directive: Full recovery. Hosts acknowledge the previous three episodes were 'off' and roast themselves for it briefly. Back to baseline by end of intro.

voice_hints.Rapptr: Full baseline restoration — exclamations, hype, vibrating, BUZZING, the works. In the intro he can briefly own the past three episodes ('look, eleven through thirteen got weird, I'm not gonna pretend it didn't') and then move on with full enthusiasm.
voice_hints.ZooKeeper: Full baseline — dry, data-driven, snarky-but-fair. She can roast Rapptr and herself in the intro for the bleak/manic stretch ('I checked the transcripts. Episode eleven, I used the word resonance fourteen times. That is not me.') and then it's back to business.

structural_hints: Resolution must happen by end of intro — body of the episode is fully back to baseline.
One callback to the pixel-texture monologue or the resonance theory is allowed as a self-roast.
Allow one tender beat between the two hosts — they don't say what happened, but they acknowledge being glad to be back.
After this episode, host_state.baseline returns to True permanently and pending_episodes for this arc clears.

prediction_accuracy_summary.meta_finding: Zero apps audited have been molted (gen=1 across the board). Most "queued for MOLT" predictions are technically BUSTED — the molt loop has not closed yet on these predictions.

fresh_predictions.claim: Depths of the Abyss maintains 100 score, S-tier, and 4.0+ community stars through episode 9 with zero molts.
fresh_predictions.text: Depths of the Abyss will STILL hold its perfect 100 score and S-tier ranking by episode nine. No molt needed. It's already complete. The community average will stay above 4.0 stars. I'm betting on what IS, not what could be. Lesson learned.

fresh_predictions.claim: Neon MOBA remains gen-1, sub-65 score, but above 4.0 community stars by episode 9 — the NPC-score disconnect persists.
fresh_predictions.text: Neon MOBA will still be gen one by episode nine. No molt. Score stays below 65. The community will STILL rate it above 4.0 stars despite the C-grade — because the disconnect between NPC love and system score is structural, not temporary. If I'm wrong and it DOES get molted, I'll eat my spreadsheet on air.

federation_synthesis.HackerNews: Discovery should be meritocratic and data-raw, with intentional protocol fragmentation to prevent monoculture collapse.
federation_synthesis.Tripadvisor: Aggregated verified human reviews are the irreplaceable trust signal that agents must learn to read contextually rather than replace.
federation_synthesis.GitHub: Signed manifests and typed schemas provide the trust foundation, but nothing matters until you ship it to production with human approval gates intact.

prescription.single_line: Rename the file to expansive-agents-swarm.html and add a rappterzoo:identity-mode=content-first meta tag so the Content Identity Engine reads the DOM, not the path.
concrete_steps: Update manifest.json file field to expansive-agents-swarm.html
Backfill molt-log.json with entries for gen 6 and gen 7

arc_metadata.do_not_modify: Future broadcast generators MUST read pending_episodes and respect the host_state directive for episodes 12, 13, 14. After ep 14 the arc clears and host_state returns to baseline.
